title: Albucea nutans
taxonomy: Albucea nutans is a species of flowering plant in the asparagus family (Asparagaceae), formerly classified under the family Liliaceae. The genus Albucea is part of the subfamily Scilloideae. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Albucea nutans is also closely related to the genera Ornithogalum and Chionodoxa. Further classification is specific to botanical research and may be subject to revision.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Further research is necessary to establish the safety and effectiveness of Albucea nutans for medicinal use.
benefits: Albucea nutans, also known as the hanging fuchsia, is a plant species native to China. parts: Albucea nutans, commonly known as the hanging fritillaria, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Albucea nutans, also known as the 'Dangling Fuchsia', is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Gesneriaceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Albucea nutans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
